qoq_changes: Revenue increased 7.0% QoQ to $47.12 billion (implied Q1 2025 revenue of $44.02 billion).
Net income decreased 32.0% QoQ to $1.89 billion (implied Q1 2025 net income of $2.78 billion).
new_risks: Specific Item 1A risk factors were omitted from the excerpt.
GM Financial long-term debt increased to $81.9 billion from $77.0 billion at year-end 2024, indicating higher leverage.
management_tone: Cautious; while GM Financial showed growth and dividends increased, core automotive revenue and overall operating income experienced significant year-over-year declines.
analyst_note: GM's Q2 2025 highlights severe margin compression, with operating income falling nearly 45% YoY despite only a slight revenue dip. Weakness in the core Automotive segment is a primary concern, though GM Financial remains a bright spot.
